title: rubus cercophyllus
intro: Rubus cercophyllus, also known as the wild briar, is a species of bramble native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus cercophyllus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
